calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT PROVIDENCE FOUNDATION OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Off calendar. The demurrer was heard on May 22, 2015. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/eg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-06-2015 Off Calendar

Defendant Providence Foundation Of San Francisco'S Demurrer (Control Date)
Off Calendar May-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sarah S. Wright, of The Wright Law Office, Tel. 415.472.5900, for plaintiffs;
Nicole L. Meredith, of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP, Tel. 415.398.0200, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Providence Foundation Of San Francisco's Demurrer to Complaint is Sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice ("RJN") is granted. Plaintiff previously filed a small claims action against Defendant seeking damages because she "was wrongfully terminated in retaliation." (RJN, Ex. A.) Plaintiff subsequently dismissed the small claims action with prejudice. (RJN, Ex. B.) "[F]or purposes of applying the doctrine of res judicata, a dismissal with prejudice is the equivalent of a final judgment on the merits, barring the entire cause of action." (Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 793.) A final small claims judgment is entitled to res judicata effect. (Pitzen v. Sup. Ct. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1374.) The cases cited by Plaintiff in opposition are inapposite, as they discuss the doctrine of collateral estoppel, not res judicata. All of the claims in this action seek redress for the same harm alleged in the small claims action - Plaintiff's termination. Per the primary rights doctrine, all of the causes of action are barred by res judicata. (See Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 798-99.). Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
